tasks.title: Prisma connection pooling + structured request logging + health check endpoint
tasks.description:
Bundle three production infrastructure items into one task:

**1. Prisma connection pooling** — Update `apps/api/.env.example` to document the `?connection_limit=20&pool_timeout=10` query parameters for DATABASE_URL. In `apps/api/src/index.ts`, after the `SELECT 1` connectivity check, log the Prisma pool configuration (connection limit, pool timeout) at startup using the existing pino logger from `apps/api/src/utils/logger.ts`. Add a `LOG_LEVEL` env var to `.env.example` and wire it into the pino logger config so production can control verbosity.

**2. Structured request logging** — Add a pino-http middleware to `apps/api/src/app.ts` that logs every request with: method, url, status code, response time (ms), content-length, and a generated request ID (uuid or nanoid). Place it early in the middleware chain (after helmet, before compression). For GraphQL requests, extract the operation name from the request body and include it in the log. Install `pino-http` as a dependency. Use the existing pino logger as the base logger. Skip logging for `/api/health` and `/api/metrics` to avoid noise.

**3. Health check endpoint** — Add `GET /api/health` in `apps/api/src/app.ts` that returns `{ status: 'ok', uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }`. Also check Prisma connectivity (try `prisma.$queryRaw(SELECT 1)` — if it fails, return 503 with `{ status: 'error', error: 'database unreachable' }`). Add it before rate limiting middleware so health checks aren't rate-limited. Update the Dockerfile to add a `HEALTHCHECK` instruction using curl to `/api/health`.
acceptance: DATABASE_URL pooling params documented in .env.example
LOG_LEVEL env var controls pino log level
Every HTTP request logs: method, url, status, responseTime, requestId
GraphQL requests include operationName in log
GET /api/health returns 200 with uptime and timestamp when DB is reachable
GET /api/health returns 503 when DB is unreachable
Health check is not rate-limited
Dockerfile has HEALTHCHECK instruction
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass
tasks.reviewNotes: STILL FAILING: pnpm typecheck gives error TS2307 Cannot find module pino-http or its corresponding type declarations at apps/api/src/app.ts(22,28). The import line is: import pinoHttpModule from pino-http. Fix option 1: run cd apps/api && pnpm add -D @types/pino-http (if types package exists). Fix option 2: the pino-http v11 should ship its own types - check if pnpm install was actually run after adding pino-http to package.json. Fix option 3: if no types exist, use require() with a .d.ts declaration file, or add a declare module pino-http in a .d.ts file. Run pnpm typecheck in the main repo to verify the fix.

tasks.title: Prometheus metrics endpoint with Prisma pool stats and request latency histograms
tasks.description:
Add a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint for production observability:

**1. Install prom-client** — Add `prom-client` to `apps/api/package.json`. Create `apps/api/src/utils/metrics.ts` that initializes the default Prometheus registry and defines the following metrics:
- `http_request_duration_seconds` — Histogram with buckets [0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5], labels: method, route, status_code
- `http_requests_total` — Counter with labels: method, route, status_code
- `graphql_resolver_duration_seconds` — Histogram with labels: resolver_name
- `prisma_pool_active_connections` — Gauge
- `prisma_pool_idle_connections` — Gauge
- `prisma_pool_wait_count` — Gauge
- `app_uptime_seconds` — Gauge (updated on scrape)
- Collect default Node.js metrics (memory, CPU, event loop) via `collectDefaultMetrics()`

**2. Metrics middleware** — In `apps/api/src/app.ts`, add middleware that records request duration and count for every request. For GraphQL requests, parse the operation name and use it as the route label (instead of just `/graphql`). Place it after the health check but before other routes.

**3. Metrics endpoint** — Add `GET /api/metrics` in `apps/api/src/app.ts` that returns `register.metrics()` with content-type `text/plain; version=0.0.4`. Place it before rate limiting so metrics scraping isn't rate-limited. For Prisma pool metrics, use `prisma.$metrics.prometheus()` if available (Prisma 5.x supports this with the `metrics` preview feature), or fall back to manual tracking.

**4. Prisma metrics preview feature** — Add `previewFeatures = ["metrics"]` to the generator block in `apps/api/prisma/schema/schema.prisma`. This enables `prisma.$metrics.prometheus()` which exposes pool stats automatically.
acceptance: prom-client installed and metrics registry initialized
GET /api/metrics returns Prometheus-format text with all defined metrics
Request duration histogram records latency for all HTTP requests
GraphQL requests are labeled by operation name
Prisma pool metrics are exposed (active, idle, wait count)
Default Node.js metrics (memory, CPU) are collected
Metrics endpoint is not rate-limited
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: Unit test foundation — Vitest setup + tests for resolverHelpers and useTaskFiltering
tasks.description:
Set up the test infrastructure and write tests for the two most critical utility modules:

**1. Vitest setup for API** — Install `vitest` as a devDependency in `apps/api/package.json`. Create `apps/api/vitest.config.ts` with TypeScript support and path aliases matching `tsconfig.json`. Add a `test` script to `apps/api/package.json`: `"test": "vitest run"`. Create `apps/api/src/__tests__/resolverHelpers.test.ts` with tests for:
- `validateStatus()` — test all valid statuses pass, invalid strings throw with correct error code
- `parseInput()` — test valid inputs pass through, invalid inputs throw GraphQL errors with Zod details
- `sanitizeForPrompt()` — test HTML/script injection is escaped, `<user_input>` tags are wrapped
- `requireProject()` / `requireTask()` — test with mock Prisma context, verify org membership check, verify not-found throws ERR_NOT_FOUND

**2. Vitest setup for Web** — Install `vitest`, `@testing-library/react`, `@testing-library/jest-dom`, and `jsdom` as devDependencies in `apps/web/package.json`. Create `apps/web/vitest.config.ts` with `environment: 'jsdom'` and path aliases. Add a `test` script. Create `apps/web/src/__tests__/useTaskFiltering.test.ts` with tests for:
- Search query filtering (case-insensitive title/description matching)
- Status filter (single status, 'all' returns everything)
- Priority filter
- Assignee filter (including 'unassigned')
- Label filter (multi-label AND/OR logic)
- Custom field filter (string matching on field values)
- Combined filters (search + status + priority simultaneously)
- Edge cases: empty task list, undefined fields, null values

Test the hook's filtering logic by extracting the filter function or testing via `renderHook` from testing-library.

**3. Root test script** — Add `"test": "pnpm -r run test"` to the root `package.json` so `pnpm test` runs all tests across both packages.
acceptance: vitest installed in both api and web packages
apps/api vitest.config.ts works with TypeScript
apps/web vitest.config.ts works with jsdom environment
resolverHelpers tests cover: validateStatus, parseInput, sanitizeForPrompt (at minimum)
useTaskFiltering tests cover: search, status, priority, assignee, combined filters (at minimum)
pnpm test runs all tests from root and they all pass
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass
tasks.reviewNotes: SECOND REJECTION: Same issue — typecheck fails with TS2307 Cannot find module vitest in apps/api/src/__tests__/resolverHelpers.test.ts(1,38). The issue is that vitest types are not visible to tsc. You MUST fix this. Concrete fix: add a triple-slash directive at the very top of the test file: /// <reference types="vitest" /> — this tells tsc where to find the types. Alternatively, add "types": ["vitest/globals"] to compilerOptions in apps/api/tsconfig.json. Do NOT just re-submit without making a change.

tasks.title: Error handling audit — replace silent catches with toast feedback + fix export.ts any types
tasks.description:
Improve error visibility and type safety across the codebase:

**1. NotificationCenter error handling** — In `apps/web/src/components/NotificationCenter.tsx`, there are 3 silent `catch { /* ignore */ }` blocks (around lines 66, 83, 99). These hide failures from users when notifications can't be fetched, marked as read, or dismissed. Import and use the toast system (from `useToast` hook or a shared toast utility). Replace each silent catch with: `catch (e) { console.error('Notification error:', e); }` — notifications are non-critical so don't show a toast, but at least log the error for debugging.

**2. GlobalSearchModal error handling** — In `apps/web/src/components/GlobalSearchModal.tsx` (around line 46), there's a silent catch on the search query. Replace with `catch (e) { setError(e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'Search failed'); }` and display the error in the UI (add a small error message below the search input).

**3. FilterBar error handling** — In `apps/web/src/components/shared/FilterBar.tsx` (around lines 77, 87), the save/delete filter operations silently catch errors. Add user feedback: import toast or set an inline error state so the user knows their filter wasn't saved/deleted.

**4. Export route type safety** — In `apps/api/src/routes/export.ts`, there are 9 `any` type annotations (lines 125-290) for Prisma query results with nested relations. Replace each `any` with proper types derived from Prisma's generated types. Use `Prisma.TaskGetPayload<{ include: { ... } }>` pattern to derive the correct types from the include clauses. Remove the `// eslint-disable-next-line` comments once the types are fixed.

**5. Dead code cleanup** — In `apps/web/src/pages/AppLayout.tsx` (lines 34-37), the `handleSSEEvent` callback is an empty function that does nothing. The SSE connection is established but events are ignored. Either remove the handler and use `useEventSource` without a callback, or add a comment explaining why it's intentionally empty and add it to a `// TODO` for future real-time event handling.
acceptance: NotificationCenter catch blocks log errors to console instead of silently swallowing
GlobalSearchModal shows error message to user when search fails
FilterBar save/delete filter operations show error feedback on failure
export.ts has zero any types — all replaced with Prisma-derived types
All eslint-disable-next-line comments for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any removed from export.ts
AppLayout SSE handler is either removed or documented with TODO
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass
tasks.reviewNotes: Task-004 code looks good but cannot merge because it shares a branch with task-003 which has a typecheck failure. Fix task-003 first (vitest types issue), then both will be re-reviewed.

tasks.title: Custom field DataLoader + add customFieldValues to TASK_FIELDS query + NUMBER/DATE filter controls
tasks.description:
Fix the N+1 query problem for custom fields and add missing filter UI:

**1. Custom field DataLoader** — In `apps/api/src/graphql/loaders.ts`, add a `customFieldValuesByTask` DataLoader that batches `customFieldValue` lookups by taskId. The loader should: `prisma.customFieldValue.findMany({ where: { taskId: { in: taskIds } }, include: { field: true } })`, then group results by taskId. In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts` (around line 527-531), replace the direct `prisma.customFieldValue.findMany` call in the `customFieldValues` field resolver with `context.loaders.customFieldValuesByTask.load(parent.taskId)`. Register the new loader in `apps/api/src/graphql/context.ts` alongside the existing loaders.

**2. Add customFieldValues to TASK_FIELDS** — In `apps/web/src/api/queries.ts`, add `customFieldValues { customFieldValueId field { customFieldId name fieldType options required position } value }` to the TASK_FIELDS constant. This ensures custom field data is fetched with every task query, enabling FilterBar to filter by custom field values without a separate request.

**3. NUMBER filter control** — In `apps/web/src/components/shared/FilterBar.tsx` (around line 212, after the TEXT case), add a NUMBER case that renders a number input with a comparison operator dropdown (=, <, >, <=, >=). Store the filter value as `operator:value` string (e.g., `>=:5`). In `apps/web/src/hooks/useTaskFiltering.ts` (around line 76-87), update the custom field filter logic to parse the `operator:value` format and do numeric comparison instead of string includes.

**4. DATE filter control** — In the same FilterBar location, add a DATE case that renders a date input. Store as `operator:date` string. In useTaskFiltering, add date comparison logic (parse ISO date strings, compare with >, <, =). For the UI, use a simple `<input type="date">` with the same operator dropdown as NUMBER.
acceptance: customFieldValuesByTask DataLoader exists and is registered in context
customFieldValues field resolver uses DataLoader instead of direct query
TASK_FIELDS includes customFieldValues with nested field data
FilterBar renders number input with operator dropdown for NUMBER custom fields
FilterBar renders date input with operator dropdown for DATE custom fields
useTaskFiltering correctly applies numeric comparisons (=, <, >, <=, >=)
useTaskFiltering correctly applies date comparisons
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: Multiple assignees — join table, GraphQL types, resolver update, multi-select assignee picker UI
tasks.description:
Full vertical slice to support multiple assignees per task:

**1. Prisma schema** — In `apps/api/prisma/schema/task.prisma`, add a `TaskAssignee` join model with fields: `id` (cuid), `taskId` (FK to Task), `userId` (FK to User), `assignedAt` (DateTime @default(now())), unique constraint on `[taskId, userId]`, indexes on both FKs. Keep the existing `assigneeId` field on Task for backwards compatibility but mark it as deprecated with a comment — it should still work for single-assignee queries. Add the `taskAssignees` relation to the Task model. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-task-assignees`.

**2. GraphQL types** — In `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/task.ts`, add `TaskAssignee` type with `id: ID!`, `user: User!`, `assignedAt: String!`. Add `assignees: [TaskAssignee!]!` field to the Task type. Add mutations: `addTaskAssignee(taskId: ID!, userId: ID!)` and `removeTaskAssignee(taskId: ID!, userId: ID!)`. Keep `assigneeId` on updateTask for backwards compatibility.

**3. Resolvers** — In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts`, add a `taskAssignees` DataLoader in `loaders.ts` that batches by taskId. Add the `assignees` field resolver on Task using the DataLoader. Add `addTaskAssignee` mutation that: validates task ownership (requireTask pattern), creates the join record (upsert to handle idempotency), creates an activity log entry, sends notification to the assigned user. Add `removeTaskAssignee` mutation with similar validation. When `updateTask` receives `assigneeId`, also create/update the join table entry for backwards compatibility.

**4. Frontend UI** — In `apps/web/src/components/taskdetail/TaskFieldsPanel.tsx`, replace the single assignee `<select>` dropdown (lines 93-108) with a multi-select assignee picker. Show currently assigned users as chips/tags with an X to remove. Add a dropdown to add new assignees. Use the `addTaskAssignee` and `removeTaskAssignee` mutations directly (not updateTask). Update `apps/web/src/hooks/useTaskCRUD.ts` to add `handleAddAssignee` and `handleRemoveAssignee` functions. Update the Task type in `apps/web/src/types.ts` to include `assignees: TaskAssignee[]`.
acceptance: TaskAssignee model exists with migration applied
assignees field on Task type returns list of TaskAssignee with user data
addTaskAssignee mutation creates join record and sends notification
removeTaskAssignee mutation removes join record
TaskFieldsPanel shows multi-select assignee picker with add/remove chips
Existing assigneeId field still works for backwards compatibility
taskAssignees DataLoader prevents N+1 queries
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass
